translations.en: Loubet
translations.zh: 盧貝

translations.en: a tall and dashing gentleman.
translations.zh: 一名高挑瀟灑之紳士。

translations.en: He greets you with an elaborate bow and flourish. "Good $T, mon ami
translations.zh: 他誇張地鞠躬揮手致意。「$T 安，吾友
translations.note: mon ami = my friend (法語)，刻意保留歐陸風

translations.en: "'Ave you come to moi for @instruction?"
translations.zh: 「汝可是來找在下@instruction（習藝）？」

translations.en: "You 'ave but to ask, mah friend."
translations.zh: 「但問無妨，吾友。」

translations.en: Loubet hands you a foil and a mask of golden mesh.
translations.zh: 盧貝遞給汝一把銳劍與一頂金網面罩。

translations.en: He shows you the correct stance, then you spar for a bit.
translations.zh: 他示範正確之架式，繼而與汝對練片刻。

translations.en: "Now ze off hand, $G... Mon dieu, you are (how you say it?) ambidextrous! Very good, mah friend."
translations.zh: 「此刻換另一手，$G……我的天，汝竟（如何說來著？）兩手皆能！甚妙，吾友。」

translations.en: M'sieur Loubet is a skilled teacher, and you feel like you've really learned something.
translations.zh: 盧貝先生實乃名師，汝確覺有所進益。

translations.en: "But no! You are too skilled, I 'ave nothing more to teach you!"
translations.zh: 「不行不行！汝身手太精，在下已無可再授！」

translations.en: "Practice? Here? Surely you jest!"
translations.zh: 「在此操練？汝定是說笑！」

translations.en: "No, come to ma salle sometime, zen we will spar, yes?"
translations.zh: 「不，改日來我之劍館，再較量，可乎？」

translations.en: "Oui, I teach ze fencing
translations.zh: 「正是，我授擊劍之藝

translations.en: here in my salle, my school."
translations.zh: 於此處——我之劍館、我之學堂。」

translations.en: "Is very difficult sometimes. Most fighters, zey have no subtlety, no finesse."
translations.zh: 「時常頗為棘手。多數武夫，無細膩、無格調。」

translations.en: He turns up his nose and sniffs aristocratically.
translations.zh: 他鼻子一抬，貴族般地嗤之以鼻。

translations.en: "Zey are not like you, mah friend! You are most talented!"
translations.zh: 「彼輩不似汝啊，吾友！汝極具天賦！」

translations.en: "But you, you look much better than that, my friend."
translations.zh: 「而汝，看來實比他人優秀，吾友。」

translations.en: Just ask
translations.zh: 儘管開口

translations.en: Come to ma salle sometime
translations.zh: 改日造訪我之劍館

translations.en: and I'll give you a @lesson!"
translations.zh: 我便為汝授一@lesson（一課）！」

translations.en: "I am M'sieur Loubet.
translations.zh: 「在下乃盧貝先生。

translations.en: "@Touche!" He wields his foil casually, lunging at an imaginary target.
translations.zh: 「@Touche（刺）中！」他閒適地揮劍，刺向虛擬之的。

translations.en: But you already know who I am, silly person!"
translations.zh: 但汝早已認得在下，可愛的傻子！」

translations.en: Much pleased to meet you,
translations.zh: 幸會幸會，

translations.en: madame
translations.zh: 夫人

translations.en: m'sieur
translations.zh: 先生

translations.en: "Zat simply means 'touch.' In fencing we score by touching ze opponent with ze foil @tip."
translations.zh: 「彼意即『擊中』。劍術中，以劍@tip（劍尖）到對手即得分。」

translations.en: "Zey are blunt on ze end, see?"
translations.zh: 「劍尖磨鈍，瞧見否？」

translations.en: "Ze first third of ze blade (near ze 'andle) is 'forte'--strong. It is good to parry with zis part of ze sword."
translations.zh: 「劍身首三分之一（近劍柄處）謂之 forte（強段）——強之意。以此段格擋最宜。」

translations.en: "Ze rest of ze blade is 'foible'--weak and flexible."
translations.zh: 「其餘部分謂之 foible（弱段）——柔而軟者。」

translations.en: "Ze foil, she is a very delicate thing. There must be much skill, yes?"
translations.zh: 「銳劍乃極纖細之物，故需精湛之技，可乎？」

translations.en: "You must 'old ze sword like a living bird:"
translations.zh: 「持劍須如握活鳥：

translations.en: "Too loose and she flies away. Too tight and she smothers. Just right, and..."
translations.zh: 「太鬆則飛、太緊則窒。恰到好處，便……」

translations.en: "She sings very sweet!" He strikes metal and the foil makes a pleasant @ringing noise.
translations.zh: 「她便歌唱甚甜！」他敲擊金屬，劍發出悅耳之@ringing（鳴響）。

translations.en: SHINGGG!
"I am ze @fencing @instructor."
translations.zh: 「在下乃@fencing（擊劍）@instructor（教師）。」

translations.en: "Au revoir!"
translations.zh: 「再會了！」
translations.note: Au revoir = goodbye (法語)，保留法腔風

translations.en: "I cannot 'elp you with zat."
translations.zh: 「此事在下幫不上忙。」

translator_notes: Loubet = 海盜窟擊劍教師，法腔英文 (Oui, ze, mah, mon ami)。中譯刻意保留外國風語感（『吾友』『先生』）。SHINGGG! 擬聲詞轉『鏘——！』
